session.assistant: I'll load the pr-feedback-classifier skill and execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by fetching the PR feedback and git diff data, then classifying the comments.

## ✅ Dignified Python Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-07 18:17:11 PT

Found 0 violations. All code changes comply with dignified-python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL (Look Before You Leap) - No EAFP control flow detected
✅ Exception Handling - Proper error boundaries at CLI level with SystemExit
✅ Path Operations - No path violations detected
✅ Import Organization - All absolute imports, no gratuitous aliases, proper ordering
✅ Keyword-only Arguments - Functions with 5+ params use `*` separator correctly
✅ CLI Patterns - Proper use of click.echo and error handling
✅ Subprocess - No subprocess violations detected
✅ Test Patterns - Appropriate default parameters in test helpers

### Violations Summary
None detected

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/codespace/setup_cmd.py`: 0 violations
- `src/erk/cli/repo_resolution.py`: 0 violations (file move, docstring update)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/check_cmd.py`: 0 violations (import reordering)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/close_cmd.py`: 0 violations (import reordering)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/duplicate_check_cmd.py`: 0 violations (import reordering)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py`: 0 violations (import reordering)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/log_cmd.py`: 0 violations (import reordering)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view_cmd.py`: 0 violations (import reordering)
- `tests/unit/cli/commands/codespace/test_setup_cmd.py`: 0 violations (test helpers)

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-07 18:17:11 PT**: No violations detected - Code refactoring maintains all dignified-python standards

</details>", "body": "<!-- dignified-code-simplifier-review -->

## ✅ Dignified Code Simplifier Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-07 18:17:20 PT

No violations found across 9 changed files.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL Exception Handling - Conditions properly checked before operations
✅ Import Organization - All absolute imports, module-level placement
✅ String Formatting - Proper use of f-strings
✅ Pathlib Usage - Correct Path operations with encoding specifications
✅ Lightweight Constructors - Factory functions with no I/O
✅ Variable Declaration - Variables declared close to use
✅ Code Clarity - Descriptive names, clear docstrings
✅ No Unnecessary Abstractions - Helper functions have clear purpose

### Violations Summary
None detected.

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/codespace/setup_cmd.py`: 0 violations
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/check_cmd.py`: 0 violations

</details>", "body": "<!-- tripwires-review -->

## ✅ Tripwires Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-07 18:17:35 PT

No tripwire violations detected. Clean refactoring PR.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Tripwires Triggered
- Universal tripwires checked (subprocess, Path.home, os.chdir, time imports, etc.)
- CLI tripwires checked (error handling, Click patterns, parameter validation)
- Testing tripwires checked (monkeypatch usage, fake patterns, test organization)

### Tier 1 Pattern Matches (Mechanical)
✅ `subprocess\.run` with `check=True` - None found
✅ `Path\.home\(\)` - None found 
✅ `os\.chdir\(\)` - None found
✅ `import time` or `datetime\.now\(\)` - None found
✅ `@patch` or `monkeypatch\.` - None found

### Tier 2 Semantic Matches (LLM-derived)
✅ Adding new CLI parameters without validation - Not applicable (no new parameters)
✅ Using RuntimeError for expected CLI failures - Not applicable (uses UserFacingCliError correctly)
✅ Modifying business logic without tests - Not applicable (refactoring only, test coverage updated)
✅ Using bare subprocess calls - Not found
✅ Using Click patterns requiring special handling - Not found

### Analysis Summary

This PR is a straightforward module relocation:
- Moved `repo_resolution.py` from `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/` to `src/erk/cli/`
- Updated 7 import statements across PR commands
- Consolidated duplicate `_resolve_owner_repo()` in codespace setup to use centralized version
- Added proper test fixtures (`RepoContext`) to maintain test coverage
- Updated error messages to use centralized function's wording

**Code Quality:**
- Uses `UserFacingCliError` for expected failures (correct pattern)
- Maintains frozen dataclass conventions 
- Follows LBYL pattern (checks before operations)
- No backwards compatibility shims (clean break per erk conventions)
- Test coverage properly updated

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/repo_resolution.py`: 0 violations (moved file, no changes to logic)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/codespace/setup_cmd.py`: 0 violations (clean refactor)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/check_cmd.py`: 0 violations (import update only)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/close_cmd.py`: 0 violations (import update only)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/duplicate_check_cmd.py`: 0 violations (import update only)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py`: 0 violations (import update only)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/log_cmd.py`: 0 violations (import update only)
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view_cmd.py`: 0 violations (import update only)
- `tests/unit/cli/commands/codespace/test_setup_cmd.py`: 0 violations (proper test updates)

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-07 18:17:35 PT**: No violations detected. Checked 3 tripwire categories (universal, CLI, testing) across 9 changed files. Clean refactoring with proper test coverage.

</details>",
